sentences.sentence: Climate change is with us.
sentences.translation: 气候变化已经来到我们身边。
difficult_points: be with us 在此表示气候变化已成为现实，而非单纯陪伴。
简短陈述为全文确立确定语气。

sentences.sentence: A decade ago, it was conjecture.
sentences.translation: 十年前，它还只是推测。
difficult_points: it 回指 climate change。
conjecture 在此作不可数概念名词，表示尚未证实的推测。

sentences.sentence: Now the future is unfolding before our eyes.
sentences.translation: 如今，未来正在我们眼前展开。
difficult_points: 现在进行时强调变化正在发生。
unfold before our eyes 是比喻，表示预测中的未来正变成眼前现实。

sentences.sentence: Canada's Inuit see it in disappearing Arctic ice and permafrost.
sentences.translation: 加拿大因纽特人从不断消失的北极海冰和永久冻土中看到了气候变化。

structure.text: in disappearing Arctic ice and permafrost

difficult_points: it 指 climate change。
see it in 表示从某种现象中辨识出气候变化。

expressions.meaning_zh: 正在消失的北极海冰与冻土

sentences.sentence: The shantytown dwellers of Latin America and Southern Asia see it in lethal storms and floods.
sentences.translation: 拉丁美洲和南亚棚户区的居民从致命风暴与洪水中感受到它。

structure.text: The shantytown dwellers of Latin America and Southern Asia

difficult_points: of Latin America and Southern Asia 修饰 shantytown dwellers。
lethal 表示风暴和洪水能够致命。

sentences.sentence: Europeans see it in disappearing glaciers, forest fires and fatal heat waves.
sentences.translation: 欧洲人从冰川消退、森林火灾和致命热浪中看到气候变化。

structure.text: in disappearing glaciers, forest fires and fatal heat waves

difficult_points: it 继续指 climate change。
介词 in 后并列三类气候影响。

sentences.sentence: Scientists see it in tree rings, ancient coral and bubbles trapped in ice cores.
sentences.translation: 科学家则从树木年轮、古代珊瑚和冰芯中封存的气泡里看到气候变化的证据。

structure.text: in tree rings, ancient coral and bubbles trapped in ice cores

difficult_points: trapped in ice cores 修饰 bubbles。
tree rings、coral 和 bubbles 都是重建古气候的证据来源。

sentences.sentence: These reveal that the world has not been as warm as it is now for a millennium or more.
sentences.translation: 这些证据表明，一千年甚至更长时间以来，全球从未像现在这样温暖。

structure.text: that the world has not been as warm as it is now for a millennium or more

difficult_points: These 回指上一句列举的自然记录。
as warm as 比较历史气温与当前气温；for 表示持续时间范围。

expressions.text: as warm as it is now

sentences.sentence: The three warmest years on record have all occurred since 1998; 19 of the warmest 20 since 1980.
sentences.translation: 有记录以来最热的三个年份都出现在1998年以后；最热的20个年份中有19个出现在1980年以后。

structure.text: The three warmest years on record

difficult_points: on record 修饰 years，表示有记录以来。
分号后省略 have occurred，完整意思是19个最热年份出现在1980年以后。

sentences.sentence: And Earth has probably never warmed as fast as in the past 30 years--a period when natural influences on global temperatures, such as solar cycles and volcanoes should have cooled us down.
sentences.translation: 而且，地球变暖的速度很可能从未像过去30年这样快——在这段时期，本来应使全球降温的自然因素包括太阳周期和火山活动。

structure.text: Earth has probably never warmed as fast as in the past 30 years
when natural influences on global temperatures, such as solar cycles and volcanoes should have cooled us down

difficult_points: as fast as 比较变暖速度；破折号后的 a period 与 the past 30 years 同位。
when 从句修饰 period；should have cooled 表示按理过去本应降温却未实现。

expressions.meaning_zh: 对全球气温的自然影响

sentences.sentence: Climatologists reporting for the UN Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) say we are seeing global warming caused by human activities.
sentences.translation: 为联合国政府间气候变化专门委员会撰写报告的气候学家表示，我们正在见证由人类活动造成的全球变暖。

structure.text: reporting for the UN Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC)
we are seeing global warming caused by human activities

difficult_points: reporting for ... 修饰 Climatologists。
say 后省略 that；caused by ... 修饰 global warming。

expressions.text: global warming caused by human activities
expressions.meaning_zh: 人类活动导致的全球变暖

sentences.sentence: People are causing the change by burning nature's vast stores of coal, oil and natural gas.
sentences.translation: 人类通过燃烧自然界蕴藏的大量煤炭、石油和天然气，正在造成这种变化。

structure.text: by burning nature's vast stores of coal, oil and natural gas

difficult_points: by + 动名词说明人类造成变化的方式。
nature's vast stores of 指自然界长期储存的大量化石燃料。

sentences.sentence: This releases billions of tonnes of carbon dioxide (CO ) every year, although the changes may actually have started with the dawn of agriculture, 2 say some scientists.
sentences.translation: 这每年释放数十亿吨二氧化碳；不过，一些科学家认为，这种变化实际上可能早在农业诞生时就已开始。

structure.text: This releases billions of tonnes of carbon dioxide (CO ) every year
although the changes may actually have started with the dawn of agriculture

difficult_points: This 指燃烧化石燃料；although 引出气候变化更早起源的补充。
may have started 表示对过去起点的推测。原文把 CO₂ 的下标 2 错置到报道语前，必须保留。

sentences.sentence: The physics of the "greenhouse effect" has been a matter of scientific fact for a century.
sentences.translation: 一个世纪以来，“温室效应”的物理原理一直是科学事实。

structure.text: has been a matter of scientific fact

difficult_points: has been 与 for a century 搭配，表示状态持续至今。
a matter of scientific fact 表示已属科学事实，而非争议观点。

sentences.sentence: CO is a greenhouse gas that traps the Sun's radiation within the troposphere, the lower atmosphere.
sentences.translation: 二氧化碳是一种温室气体，会把太阳辐射困在对流层，也就是大气低层。

structure.text: that traps the Sun's radiation within the troposphere

difficult_points: that 从句修饰 greenhouse gas。
the lower atmosphere 与 the troposphere 同位。原文 CO 缺少下标 2，须保留。

sentences.sentence: 2 It has accumulated along with other manmade greenhouse gases, such as methane and chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs).
sentences.translation: 它与甲烷、氯氟烃等其他人造温室气体一道不断累积。

structure.text: along with other manmade greenhouse gases

difficult_points: It 指二氧化碳；along with 连接一同累积的其他气体。
句首 2 是上一句 CO₂ 下标的错置残留，必须原样保留。

sentences.sentence: Some studies suggest that cosmic rays may also be involved in warming.
sentences.translation: 一些研究表明，宇宙射线也可能参与了变暖过程。

structure.text: that cosmic rays may also be involved in warming

difficult_points: that 从句作 suggest 的宾语。
may be involved in 表示可能参与，但不等于确定因果。

sentences.sentence: . If current trends continue, we will raise atmospheric CO concentrations to double pre-industrial levels during this century.
sentences.translation: 如果当前趋势持续下去，本世纪大气中的二氧化碳浓度将被提高到工业化前水平的两倍。

structure.text: we will raise atmospheric CO concentrations

difficult_points: 段首孤立句点是源文异常，为保持段落完整性并入本句。
If 从句表示趋势持续的条件；double 在此限定目标浓度为两倍。原文 CO 缺下标。

sentences.sentence: That will probably be enough 2 to raise global temperatures by around 2 to 5.
sentences.translation: 这可能足以使全球气温上升约2至5度。

structure.text: 2 to raise global temperatures by around 2 to 5

difficult_points: That 回指二氧化碳浓度翻倍。
原文 enough 后的 2 是错置字符，且 2 to 5 后缺温度单位，必须原样保留。

sentences.sentence: Some warming is certain, but the degree will be determined by cycles involving melting ice, the oceans, water vapour, clouds and changes to vegetation.
sentences.translation: 一定程度的变暖已不可避免，但具体幅度将由涉及冰融化、海洋、水蒸气、云层和植被变化的循环过程决定。

structure.text: but the degree will be determined by cycles
involving melting ice, the oceans, water vapour, clouds and changes to vegetation

difficult_points: but 对比变暖确定性与具体幅度的不确定性。
involving ... 修饰 cycles，列出反馈循环涉及的因素。

sentences.sentence: Warming is bringing other unpredictable changes.
sentences.translation: 变暖还在带来其他无法预测的变化。
difficult_points: Warming 是名词化的现在分词，作主语。
现在进行时强调这些连锁变化正在发生。

sentences.sentence: Melting glaciers and precipitation are causing some rivers to overflow, while evaporation is emptying others.
sentences.translation: 冰川融化和降水使一些河流泛滥，而蒸发又使另一些河流干涸。

structure.text: are causing some rivers to overflow

difficult_points: cause + 宾语 + to do 表示导致河流溢出。
while 对比两种相反水文结果；others 代替 other rivers。

expressions.meaning_zh: 而蒸发使另一些河流干涸

sentences.sentence: Diseases are spreading.
sentences.translation: 疾病正在传播。
difficult_points: 现在进行时说明疾病传播是正在发展的气候影响。
句子未限定具体疾病，表达总体趋势。

sentences.sentence: Some crops grow faster while others see yields slashed by disease and drought.
sentences.translation: 一些作物生长更快，而另一些作物的产量则因疾病和干旱而锐减。

structure.text: while others see yields slashed by disease and drought

difficult_points: while 对比不同作物受变暖影响的结果；others 指 other crops。
see + 宾语 + 过去分词表示经历产量被大幅削减。

expressions.meaning_zh: 遭遇因……导致的产量锐减

sentences.sentence: Clashes over dwindling water resources may cause conflicts in many regions.
sentences.translation: 围绕日益减少的水资源发生的争夺，可能在许多地区引发冲突。
difficult_points: over 表示冲突围绕的对象。
dwindling 是现在分词形容词，表示水资源不断减少。

sentences.sentence: As natural ecosystems - such as coral reefs - are disrupted, biodiversity is reduced.
sentences.translation: 随着珊瑚礁等自然生态系统受到破坏，生物多样性也随之下降。

structure.text: As natural ecosystems - such as coral reefs - are disrupted

difficult_points: As 引导时间兼原因关系；破折号内举例说明 ecosystems。
are disrupted 与 is reduced 均为被动语态。

sentences.sentence: Most species cannot migrate fast enough to keep up, though others are already evolving in response to warming.
sentences.translation: 大多数物种无法足够快地迁移以跟上变化，不过另一些物种已开始通过进化应对变暖。

structure.text: Most species cannot migrate fast enough

difficult_points: fast enough to ... 表示速度足以实现某结果，此处否定为跟不上。
though 引出少数物种的不同反应；others 指 other species。

sentences.sentence: Thermal expansion of the oceans, combined with melting ice on land, is also raising sea levels.
sentences.translation: 海洋热膨胀，加上陆地冰层融化，也正在推高海平面。

structure.text: combined with melting ice on land

difficult_points: 主语中心是单数 expansion，因此谓语用 is。
combined with ... 是插入的过去分词短语，补充另一因素。

sentences.sentence: In this century, human activity could trigger an irreversible melting of the Greenland ice sheet.
sentences.translation: 在本世纪，人类活动可能触发格陵兰冰盖不可逆转的融化。

structure.text: could trigger an irreversible melting of the Greenland ice sheet

difficult_points: could 表示可能性而非确定预测。
irreversible 修饰 melting，强调一旦发生便无法恢复。

sentences.sentence: This would condemn the world to a rise in sea level of six metres - enough to flood land occupied by billions of people.
sentences.translation: 这将使全球注定面临海平面上升6米的后果——足以淹没数十亿人居住的土地。

structure.text: would condemn the world to a rise in sea level of six metres

difficult_points: This 回指格陵兰冰盖不可逆融化；condemn ... to 表示使其注定承受。
enough to ... 说明六米上升的后果，occupied by ... 修饰 land。

sentences.sentence: The global warming would be more pronounced if it were not for sulphur particles and other pollutants that shade us, and because forests and oceans absorb around half of the CO we produce.
sentences.translation: 如果没有硫颗粒及其他为地球遮挡阳光的污染物，并且如果森林和海洋没有吸收人类排放的约一半二氧化碳，全球变暖会更加明显。

structure.text: The global warming would be more pronounced
if it were not for sulphur particles and other pollutants
and because forests and oceans absorb around half of the CO we produce

difficult_points: if it were not for 是虚拟条件，表示这些因素实际存在并减缓变暖。
that 修饰 pollutants；we produce 是省略 that 的定语从句修饰 CO。
原文 and because 与前面的 if 结构不完全平行，且 CO 缺下标，均保留。

sentences.sentence: But the accumulation rate of atmospheric CO has doubled since 2001, suggesting 2 that nature's ability to absorb the gas could now be 2 stretched to the limit.
sentences.translation: 但自2001年以来，大气二氧化碳的累积速度已经翻倍，这表明自然吸收这种气体的能力如今可能已接近极限。

structure.text: the accumulation rate of atmospheric CO
suggesting 2 that nature's ability to absorb the gas could now be 2 stretched to the limit

difficult_points: has doubled 与 since 2001 搭配，表示累积速度翻倍并持续。
suggesting ... 是结果分词短语；be stretched to the limit 表示能力被逼近极限。
两个 2 都是 CO₂ 下标错置造成的源文异常。

sentences.sentence: Recent research suggests that natural CO "sinks", like peat bogs and forests, are actually starting to release 2 CO . 2
sentences.translation: 近期研究表明，泥炭沼泽和森林等天然二氧化碳“汇”实际上已开始释放二氧化碳。

structure.text: that natural CO "sinks", like peat bogs and forests, are actually starting to release 2 CO . 2

difficult_points: that 从句作 suggests 的宾语；like ... 插入举例说明 natural sinks。
start to release 表示这些碳汇开始反向释放气体。原文多个 2 均为 CO₂ 下标错置，须保留。

sentences.sentence: At the Earth Summit in 1992, the world agreed to prevent "dangerous" climate change.
sentences.translation: 在1992年地球峰会上，世界各国同意防止“危险的”气候变化。
difficult_points: the world 是转喻，指参与峰会的各国。
agree to do 表示同意采取行动。

sentences.sentence: The first step was the 1997 Kyoto Protocol, which came into force during 2005.
sentences.translation: 第一步是1997年通过的《京都议定书》，该议定书于2005年生效。

structure.text: The first step was the 1997 Kyoto Protocol

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 Kyoto Protocol。
come into force 是法规或协议“生效”的固定表达。

sentences.sentence: It will bring modest emission reductions from industrialised countries.
sentences.translation: 它将促使工业化国家实现幅度有限的减排。
difficult_points: It 指 Kyoto Protocol。
modest 表示减排幅度有限，而非态度谦虚。

sentences.sentence: Many observers say deeper cuts are needed and developing nations, which have large and growing populations, will one day have to join in.
sentences.translation: 许多观察人士说，需要进行更大幅度的减排，而人口规模庞大且持续增长的发展中国家终有一天也必须参与。

structure.text: which have large and growing populations
will one day have to join in

difficult_points: say 后有两个由 and 连接的宾语分句，省略 that。
which 修饰 developing nations；join in 表示加入减排行动。

sentences.sentence: Some, including the US Bush administration, say the scientific uncertainty over the pace of climate change is grounds for delaying action.
sentences.translation: 一些人——包括美国布什政府——认为，气候变化速度在科学上的不确定性构成了推迟行动的理由。

structure.text: the scientific uncertainty over the pace of climate change is grounds for delaying action

difficult_points: including ... 补充说明 Some 包括哪些主体。
say 后省略 that；grounds for 后接动名词，表示做某事的理由。

sentences.sentence: The US and Australia have reneged on Kyoto.
sentences.translation: 美国和澳大利亚已经背弃了《京都议定书》的承诺。
difficult_points: renege on 表示背弃承诺或协议。
Kyoto 在此用协议签署地简称《京都议定书》。

sentences.sentence: Most scientists believe we are under-estimating the dangers.
sentences.translation: 大多数科学家认为，我们正在低估这些危险。
difficult_points: believe 后省略 that。
现在进行时表示低估风险是当前持续存在的问题；原文 under-estimating 带连字符。

sentences.sentence: In any case, according to the IPCC, the world needs to quickly improve the efficiency of its energy usage and develop renewable non-carbon fuels like: wind, solar, tidal, wave and perhaps nuclear power.
sentences.translation: 无论如何，IPCC认为，全球需要迅速提高能源使用效率，并发展风能、太阳能、潮汐能、波浪能以及可能包括核能在内的可再生非碳能源。

structure.text: to quickly improve the efficiency of its energy usage
like: wind, solar, tidal, wave and perhaps nuclear power

difficult_points: needs 后接两个并列不定式 improve 和 develop。
the world 是转喻，指各国社会。原文 like 后又加冒号，需保留。

expressions.text: improve the efficiency of energy usage

sentences.sentence: It also means developing new methods of converting this clean energy into motive power, like hydrogen fuel cells for cars.
sentences.translation: 这也意味着要开发把清洁能源转化为动力的新方法，例如汽车用氢燃料电池。

structure.text: of converting this clean energy into motive power
like hydrogen fuel cells for cars

difficult_points: means 后接动名词 developing；It 指实现减排目标。
convert A into B 表示把清洁能源转化为动力。

sentences.sentence: Other less conventional solutions include ideas to stave off warming by "mega-engineering" the planet with giant mirrors to deflect the Sun's rays, seeding the oceans with iron to generate algal blooms, or burying greenhouse gases below the sea.
sentences.translation: 其他较非常规的方案包括：通过“超级工程”改造地球来阻止变暖，例如用巨型镜面偏转太阳光、向海洋撒铁以引发藻华，或把温室气体埋到海底。

structure.text: include ideas to stave off warming
with giant mirrors to deflect the Sun's rays
seeding the oceans with iron to generate algal blooms
or burying greenhouse gases below the sea

difficult_points: to stave off ... 修饰 ideas，by + 动名词说明手段。
seeding 与 burying 同 mega-engineering 构成并列方案；两个 to 不定式分别说明目的。

sentences.sentence: The bottom line is that we will need to cut CO emissions by 70% to 80% simply to stabilise atmospheric CO concentrations--and thus 2 temperatures.
sentences.translation: 归根结底，我们需要把二氧化碳排放削减70%至80%，才能稳定大气二氧化碳浓度，进而稳定气温。

structure.text: that we will need to cut CO emissions by 70% to 80%
simply to stabilise atmospheric CO concentrations--and thus 2 temperatures

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明 bottom line 的具体结论。
to stabilise 表示大幅减排的目的，and thus 连接进一步稳定气温的结果。
原文 CO 均缺下标且 2 错置在 temperatures 前，须保留。

sentences.sentence: The quicker we do that, the less unbearably hot our 2 future world will be.
sentences.translation: 我们越快做到这一点，未来世界令人难以忍受的炎热程度就越低。

structure.text: the less unbearably hot our 2 future world will be

difficult_points: the + 比较级，the + 比较级 表示“越……就越……”。
that 指大幅削减排放。原文 our 与 future 之间的 2 是 CO₂ 下标错置残留。
